chapter_title.ar: باب الْمُتَوَفَّى عَنْهَا زَوْجُهَا الْمَدْخُولِ بِهَا أَيْنَ تَعْتَدُّ وَ مَا يَجِبُ عَلَيْهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 45 – The woman widowed from her husband who has been slept with, where should she wait from and what is Obligated upon her

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/200)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/200)
text: سُئِلَ عَنِ الْمَرْأَةِ يَمُوتُ عَنْهَا زَوْجُهَا أَ يَصْلُحُ لَهَا أَنْ تَحُجَّ أَوْ تَعُودَ مَرِيضاً قَالَ نَعَمْ تَخْرُجُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَ لَا تَكْتَحِلُ وَ لَا تَطَيَّبُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammd, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘He<sup>asws</sup> was asked about the woman whose husband died, would it be correct for her that she performs Hajj, or visits to console the sick?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, she can go out in the Way of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and she should not apply Kohl, nor perfume’.
en.sarwar: 9. Muhammad ibn Ahmad has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus from a man who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ibrahim, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a woman who is widowed; if she can count her waiting period in a house for a month or less or more then move to another house and stay there as she did in the other house and so on until her waiting period is complete. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is permissible and it is not harmful.’”
